11111111111111111111.content:
***


The four of them took up almost half a bleacher.

It was easy enough to spot them. They were the talk of just about every event that they lumbered their way into. With both daughters leading the (steadily improving) soccer team, another in cheer, and the other in Art Club, there were plenty of events for them to thunder through.

“Excuse us.”

“Sorry, hun.”

“One side, y’all.”

There had never been so much motion between so few people. Watching them wade and waddle up the stands, sweating in the Autumn heat like a herd of heifers was like a car accident—that much gold and white ought to have been a crime.

Crammed into matching jerseys were the Mega Moms of Milton High—as they’d come to be known as within certain circles. All that jiggling gold on display, shimmering in the orangey brown sun, it would have been so much harder not to see them. Yes, Frumpy Flo—not Florence—Folly, Dumpy Dillon Duncan, Super-Sized Shelby Sullivan, and Colossal Carrie Cooleyfinger had still been the talk of various parental social circles, but for very different reasons than they had been less than a graduating class ago.

The four of them were huge—easily the fattest mothers in the school district. Probably in the county. And it had happened so quickly too; all before the oldest of their kids even graduated!

Sitting down, a heifer shy of a total ton of milfy meat between them, the bleacher started to buckle underneath their overwhelming weight. Bellies brushed against the lip of cold steel as they hung towards the concrete flooring, rolling towards the ground as each queen-sized keister slowly lowered down to the metal with grumbling grunts of exertion.

The shortest and roundest of the bunch, Flo, had become famous for a faux pas at an Art Club showing where she’d ripped her sundress. Ever since, she’d stuck to supporting behind the scenes where her dignity was intact. Her pixie features buried underneath inches of chub, Flo was far more well known for her belly-heavy physique than she was for her work in leading her local gallery’s nonprofits.

Dillon, the tallest and “skinniest” out of the four, had had it the roughest. Her daughter was the cheerleading captain and looking up at her meaty mama in the stands you never would have known it. The woman was shaped like a bowling pin with brown hair, her ass spreading wide over bleachers as she sweltered in the sun at this event or that. Under her volunteering and chaperoning, the Bake Club had never been more profitable—or more delicious. With all her insulation, Dillon was undoubtedly the heaviest of the bunch, and if it weren’t for her height, she might have looked it. But as it was, lowering herself to the far right of her other three fatty friends, she’d have to be content being the skinny one out of the bunch for now.

Shelby Sullivan certainly couldn’t contend—not with that saggy pear physique of hers. With how quickly she’d blown up, no one really blamed her husband for the divorce. She managed to get a settlement almost as sizable as her ass—one that had ensured that a simple desk job would be enough to afford a modicum of comfort even as she continued to swell out and out. Once upon a time one of the most sought-after mothers around, she’d gone and let herself go big, wide, and hard. She couldn’t walk fifty feet without stopping to catch her breath, lugging around more than a hundred and fifty pounds of ass behind her. Her hips catching at the sides of her jersey, a loud RIP sounded through the stands as she settled her acre of ass down on the bending bleachers beneath her.

“Whoop.” Carrie said huskily as she patted herself down, almost eager to take the credit for that one, “Musta been me.”

Carrie Cooleyfinger—easily the fattest mother out of the bunch—send her shape shimmying as she slowly sat down. The whole bench seemed to groan at what had to have been the final straw in a lifetime of abuse. The woman was huge—a blob with brown hair. Her stomach sagged low to the ground and threatened to brush against the bald head of the dad unlucky enough to sit in front of her. She’d blown up so much, so fast, she’d practically become the talk of the town! Her fat face beset in a puddle of chins and cheeks, it was all she could do most days to keep herself upright and moving!

“Must have been.” Shelby agreed as she pat her friend on the crest of her stomach, “We’re gonna have to size you up soon enough.”

“Soon enough.” Carrie agreed with a huff, her fat face blotchy and red, “Hey Dillon, how many X’s can you manage in that shop of yours?”

“Not enough to carry you through the season, I can tell you that much.” The tall cheer mom’s double chins bunch as she talked, belly wobbling as she wriggled into place, “Unless you want me to just start printing on you instead of jerseys.”

“I think we’d get kicked out.” Carrie chortled, grabbing a handful of her gut and giving it a good shake, “No one wants to see all this on display.”

“Like you give them much of a choice.” Shelby smacked Carrie’s gut from the top

“Let ‘em look.” Carrie huffed, her immense legs spreading a wide berth to allow her gut to pour over, “Who have I got to impress?”

“Does anyone want snacks?” Flo asked helpfully, “I’m starting to get a little hungry.”

“Ooh, snacks, yas.” Dillon smacked her lips appreciatively, “I could go for a chili dog.”

“Make that two chili dogs.” Shelby held up an arm as thick as most women’s thighs, “I skipped lunch.”

“You did not.”

“I had a light lunch.”

“You did not!” Dillon leaned over to smack her friend’s quivering fat thigh, “I was there!”

Carrie leaned back on the bleachers, her hands resting comfortably on her shelf of a stomach as it billowed out in front of her. As her doughy white physique eeked further and further out from underneath the tent-sized jersey that her friend had custom-ordered for her, she couldn’t’ help but think that a chili-dog sounded pretty good right about now.

“Four chili dogs for me, please.” Carrie flashed a smile, “I’ve got a figure to keep up.”

And a reputation to maintain—these women were going to give her a run as the Fat Friend of their social circle one way or the other!
11111111111111111111.title: The Fattest Moms of Milton High (pt2)

choices: Dillon's lazy daughter D.W. lives at home, and enables her mama's worst habits..
Flo's overreliance on The Help is starting to make her pretty roly-poly...
Shelby's substituting food for sex is going to make her the size of a house...
111111111111111111111.content:
“Flat tummies are for married gals.”

The two women would often raise their wine glasses together in solidarity over their divorces. It was as close to clinking them together as either of them could get, with how much of themselves happened to be between them.

“Here here.”

In the fleshy foursome of their friend group, only Shelby and Carrie were without husbands. The former divorced and living high and wide off of a substantial alimony check while the latter was fairly successful on her own, they shared a unique status among their friends as the only truly “independent” women between them.

Sometimes, when Flo and Dillon were busy (and sometimes, even when they weren’t) Shelby and Carrie would spend their afternoons together, just the two of them. They didn’t do anything that was particularly different than what the four of them typically did together, which was to gorge themselves stupid and round until they could barely waddle out of whatever eatery they’d wedged themselves into, but somehow it seemed much more intimate and friendly when it was just the two of them—something that Shelby, at least, appreciated.

When the two of them had become acquainted, what seemed like an age and a half ago, they had both been thin and particularly attractive. Both of their girls were still in high school, instead of getting ready to graduate college. They were both much more active in the community, and had more booming social lives that ventured outside of local restaurants and venues.
Most importantly, and whether or not Shelby cared to remember this particularly heinous plot, Carrie had been unwittingly labeled as the group’s Designated Ugly Fat Friend—with a plot to make her fit the bill by fattening her up so that they all (particularly Shelby) looked better by comparison.

In the many years since, it had become difficult to believe that there had ever been a time when she could have looked slimmer when compared to any one of her three friends; up to and including Carrie Cooleyfinger.

“Carrie, hun, would you pass me a biscuit?”

“Sure thing sweetie.”

Carrie had learned over the course of many of these one on one meals with Shelby that what she had really meant was for her to pass her the wicker basket full of biscuits. She would pick from the lot, three or four usually, and scarf them down over the course of the next few sips of wine and bites of her main course. With so many snacks to choose from, you would have thought that the biscuits would have lasted longer, but then you’d be wrong.

Shelby’s sausage fingers wrapped around a biscuit, and then another and two more, as she placed them aside. Her fleshy arms could only reach so far and hold so much, after all. But with her pathetically shortened wingspan, she managed to keep herself obscenely well-fed, even without Carrie’s help. The maids would attest to that.

“Theef are to die for.” Shelby’s fat cheeks and rolling jowls wobbled as she gushed, “I simply can’t get enough of them.”

“Yeah, no kidding.” Carrie laughed shallowly as she pet her bloated stomach, “I think I’m gonna turn into a biscuit at this rate.”

“You’d make a wonderful biscuit, hun.” Shelby sniffed, “I would eat you in a heartbeat.”

The Shelby Sullivan sitting across from her was about five or six times the woman that she’d been when they’d met at their daughters’ soccer game all that time ago. The stunning redheaded MILF that had turned so many heads now did it as an avalanche of pizza dough, and for much different reasons than when she had been on the PTA.

Carrie would have never thought poorly of her friend—after all, she and Dillon and Flo were about the best friends that a gal like her could ask for—but it was getting harder and harder for her to reconcile the image of the woman in front of her with the one that she (faintly) recalled as this awesome, take-no-shit, modern debutante that had caught her eye from all the way across the bleachers…

Just when had they all started getting so freaking fat?

“Ooh… butter…” Shelby’s words rumbled thickly in her ring of chins, “Pass the butter, hun…”

Carrie happily obliged her friend as she struggled to lean over the impossible sandbag that was her own stomach. Her humongous hips weighing her down meant that, even as she stretched out her pathetically chubby arms and wriggled her little fingers, she didn’t lift so much as a centimeter off of the overloaded piece of furniture beneath her.

And as the self-aware “Fat Friend” of their foursome, Carrie still wore the badge with pride. It was all in good fun and, after all, how many of them could really hope to hold onto their girlish figure once they reached a certain age?

Plus, it meant that she especially didn’t have to worry about overeating and plumping out—at least, not in the sense that she had to worry about her friends talking about her behind her back. It was freeing, that way! Even if it was just a running joke between friends, Carrie had become confident enough in herself that it didn’t bother her that Dillon, Flo, and (especially) Shelby used her as an excuse to eat as much as they wanted.

If anything, given the disparity between their respective budgets, it meant that they could do more together—no going to fancy parties where Carrie wouldn’t fit in, no social obligations that she would have had to stress out about… just parking themselves at various restaurants and just enjoying some good food and better company.

Though, with how quickly everyone around her seemed to be blowing up, Carrie could muse only idly about how long it had been since “Fat Friend” was anything more than a title—now almost entirely arbitrary by comparison…
111111111111111111111.title: "Fat Friend" by Comparison

choices: Supper at Shelby's becomes a regular occurrence...
Shelby's going to have to be creative to get this ball rolling...
1111112.content:
After Troy had greeted them at the door, he ushered his wife’s friend Carrie and her daughter to their seats at the table. An already uncorked bottle of red wine was there, with Shelby waiting for her friend glass in hand, as the two of them became said their hellos.

However, while Carrie was preoccupied getting to know her daughter’s new friend, Shelby had excused herself deeper into the kitchen to begin serving dinner. She’d finished her glass of wine, set it aside, and put both hands on the mashed potatoes she’d slaved over making the “right” way. No butter, no salt, no cheese. Boring as all get out—but that was just one of the ways she could keep Troy off nagging about her weight.

“Your friend’s nice.” Troy said as an aside, finishing his bottle of beer and tossing it into the trashcan by the door, “Real handsome woman, too.”

“Mm.” Shelby pursed her lips as she lifted the boring potatoes, her expression as flat and loveless as their sex life, “Eats like a cow though.”

“She might eat like a cow, but at least she puts it in the right places.” Troy said the words loosely with a little chuckle, “Nothin’ compared to yer gal Flo though—I wouldn’t kick her out of bed for eatin’ biscuits, that much is for sure.”

The synapses between Shelby’s gray matter seemed to tickle at yet another mention of one of her oldest, and apparently prettiest friends. Troy had made plenty of comments in the past before about Flo Folly—Artsy Mom of their little power trio—but to bring her up when she wasn’t even around? When she wasn’t even part of the conversation? That rubbed her through the callus and right into the raw.

“Now is that any way to talk about our guest—that she’s not as pretty as little ol’ Flo?” Shelby managed as fireworks flew behind her eyes, “If you’re not careful you might hurt somebody’s feelings.”

“Well then don’t tell her that I think Dillon’s prettier’n both of y’all put together.” Troy winked and held a finger over his lips, a little buzzed and especially loose lipped after one too many beers, “It’ll make dinner conversation real awkward.”

An angry growl escaped the stoic Southern mom as her husband slunk into the next room to play nice with Carrie. How could he be such a pig? Talking about her friends that way when all he had to say about his own wife of almost eighteen years was that she was getting fat?

Well, if he was so worried about his wife’s waistline—maybe she’d give him a better perspective. Show him what some real cows looked like…

***


“Go ahead and eat up, y’all!” Shelby said with a practiced friendliness and a subdued southern spite, worthy of any hostess on the Food Network, “I made plenty—no reason to be shy!”

No sooner than had the words left her lips that her guests of the evening started to dig in. And who could blame them? Shelby was easily the best cook out of the four of them, and she didn’t break out the kitchenware near often enough for their liking. Nobody quite knew what the bug up her butt was, but she’d gotten a real kick to play Hostess lately. She’d been inviting the girls over just about every other day for dinner, making a spread fit for a queen (or four queens, in this case) and keeping everyone’s glasses filled with sweet tea and marmalade wine.

“Thank you guys again for working around my schedule.” Carrie addressed the group as she helped herself to a heap of green beans from the dish, “I really appreciate it.”

“Oh, it’s no problem at all, Carrie honey.” Shelby pat her friend on the cheek with one long ivory hand, “Besides, I don’t think you’ll hear either of them girls over there complaining about comin’ over to Shelby’s for supper!”

The deck of the Sullivan’s plantation home was alive and well, even in the early Autumn darkness. The mason jar lights were lit, and the new patio table was fit to bust at the sizable spread of southern cooking Shelby’d loaded up. Pot roast with mashed potatoes, green beans, and a macaroni pie recipe she’d stolen from Pinterest. Still chilling in the fridge were two pies (lemon and key lime), while another basketful of biscuits cooled on the countertop in the kitchen—the whole nine yards, as far as southern get-togethers went.

What’s more, Shelby knew all of their favorites. She made plenty of them just about every night.

“Y’all are gonna need a forklift to get me outta here.” Dillon said with a little laugh as she helped herself to a mighty plop of pot roast, “Shelby’s gonna need to take out part of the fence before she’s done with us!”

“This macaroni pie is to die for, sugar~” Flo crooned after her first bite, “Carrie, honey, you want some of this?”

“Do I.” Carrie said greedily, fumbling with the plate as the wine started to hit, “I’ve never had anything this good!”

The redheaded hostess could only smile to herself, besetting a certain terrible triumph in the dimpling of her cheeks. Moving their little get-togethers to the porch had been the best idea she’d ever had. Instead of climbing up and down the stairs, keeping herself on her feet (or worse yet, her friends!), the kitchen was just a hop and a jump away. The living room was right there through the back door, too! So, when everyone was done, their bellies full of Shelby’s rich southern cooking and sloshing with wine, they could just go lay out on the long couches until their heads cleared up enough to make the drive home. And while they were there, Shelby could always count on passing out cornbread to soak up the alcohol…

“Now ain’t this so much better than that dumb ol’ sitting room?” Shelby goaded her friends, passing out biscuits in rotation, “Fresh air, lots of room to wiggle, and—”

“Great food.” Dillon said again, “I don’t understand why you ain’t been cookin’ for us like this the whole time, darlin’!”

“Well…” Shelby clicked her tongue as her smile spread across her face, “…Lots of time to make up for, then.”
1111112.title: Why Settle for One Fat Friend?

choices: It doesn't take long for the other moms to start packing it on...
Shelby can't help but steal some snacks for herself...
Free Choice
11111121.content:
As strange as it sounded, Carrie gave her the perfect excuse to keep their get-togethers as late possible.

Being the only one who had a full forty-hour work week to attend to, Shelby made a pretty big fuss about including her. After all, they were friends, right? And good friends like them did everything together—it just wasn’t fair that Carrie couldn’t come because she lived in a one-income household.

That meant that just about every time they saw one another, it was dinnertime. And since Shelby was so keen on inviting everyone over, what had once been a myriad of events around town and at the school soon became just three or four blocks a week in everyone’s schedule called “Supper at Shelby’s”.

There wouldn’t be much meeting up outside of that time, either—Shelby saw to it! Sure, she and Flo would go for a coffee now and then. Anything to get her out of that dull gallery. And maybe, if she was in the area, she’d drop by Dillon’s screen-printing shop with donuts. Her friend sure appreciated the opportunity to get out of her dinky little office. Even Carrie got in a lunch at least once a week! Somewhere quick though, since she barely got an hour.

Any other time that Shelby didn’t approve, she could always whip out the now tried-and-true excuse of “Well, I was gonna get ready for dinner tonight…”

And slowly, Dillon and Flo got the message. Their days of running around the county or showing up at the school, checking out new shops or this and that in downtown were very slowly replaced with the humdrum life that they so desperately wanted to avoid—exactly why they used to spend so much time away! Being stuck in their respective workspaces all day with nothing better to do was boring. And that only served to make Supper at Shelby’s all the more important to them. It might have been a little dull but there was always so much to look forward to…

“I was thinking maybe we could go to that football game in Mauldin.” Dillon grunted as she rolled her weight onto the armrest of the couch downstairs, her round belly nestled between her thick thighs, “I know it’s a long drive, but our boys deserve the support. They’re real good this year.”

“I don’t know, hun, that sounds like a lot.” Shelby clicked, heading into the living room with a freshly cooled sheet of cookies, “And besides, Carrie’d probably have to work the next day.”

“Well, I mean, I could ask of—” Carrie’s intrusion was cut short by a cookie being popped past her lips, “Mmph!”

“Oh, we could try that new restaurant that opened up?” Flo added helpfully, setting her glass down and rubbing the taut dome of her belly appreciatively as it bubbled and churned, “That’s something we could all do in one night.”

“There’s an idea!” Shelby congratulated her porky blonde friend with a cookie, “See, that’s something we can manage.”

“I guess…” Dillon shifted in her seat, the tightness of her (now buttonless) jeans wearing angry red lines on her supple shape, “I’m just bored y’all. It just feels like we used to do a lot more, y’know? Now it’s just… I dunno, here and home games.”

Shelby cut her eyes menacingly at the oblivious Dillon. She’d been talking a bit too much about getting out and about for Shelby’s taste. Ever since she’d started this whole “fatten up her friends” thing, she hadn’t heard hide nor hair about how hot they were from her husband—she wasn’t about to let them get adventurous and slip out of her fingers. Not when she had them right where she wanted them.

“You know what you need, hun?” Shelby said sweetly, “A spa day—we’ll all have one. Carrie can come too!”

“Really?” Carrie huffing as she rolled into a sitting position, her turgid stomach bunching awkwardly, “This weekend?”

“Next weekend.” Shelby corrected, “Y’all are still coming over for drinks on Saturday, right?”

Of course they were. Shelby’s house was the place to be. And when they wobbled through the door, there would be plenty of cookies, cakes, and pies to whet their whistle with.
11111121.title: Supper at Shelby's

choices: Things, predictably, escalate from here
Shelby is happy to give preferential treatment
111111211.content:
Carrie huffed and puffed as she lugged herself up the long long long walkway that lead up to her place of work. For a job that made her spend so much time on her ass, you would have thought that they would have put the door closer to the goddamn parking lot. But instead, it only seemed like it got longer every week! Carrie knew that she was getting out of shape, but it was the days like this—where the commute from her Honda Civic to the front door left her winded—that she really told herself that she was getting too fat for her own good.

But what was the harm? Her friends were fat—God that was such a mean thing to say.

It was true, though! How could anyone expect a Southern single mother to keep her figure in a state where there were almost literally no gyms or sidewalks? It was a miracle she’d kept ahold of her figure for this long…

Even still, getting dressed for work was getting harder. Not just in finding clothes her size, but in actually dressing herself. Carrie hadn’t seen her toes in months and bending over to pull up her slacks was becoming a herculean task—even when she wasn’t still buzzed or full from the night before. All of her nights over at Shelby’s were taking their toll alright, but she was having so much with her new friends! Maybe she was getting a little round, but it wasn’t like things were out of control yet.

She’d join a gym—next month, after she’d paid the bills, of course—and maybe start skipping lunch at work. Surely that would help her shed the extra couple of… dozen pounds she’d picked up this past year?

***


Meanwhile, clear across town, the only huffing and puffing in the HVAC Art Gallery came from the locomotive exhibit upstairs. Flo Folly had spent all morning on her butt, propped up at the front desk with nothing better to do but scroll through Facebook.

Days like this made her miss when she and her friends did stuff! It had been months since she’d had to “rearrange” her schedule last minute because Shelby found something fun for them to do. For months now, it felt like she’d been staring at the art—not even the art, the white walls behind it—with nothing else to do.

Except eat, of course.

Flo’s fleshy folds bunched and rolled as she shifted awkwardly on her stool. Her ass was too big on either side to really sit comfortably on it anymore—how had it ever, with how small this damn thing was? Picking at the waistband of her leggings from the back, Flo reduced the half-foot of whale tail rolling out the back to a mere inch or two. She made a little grumble, her round face creasing in consternation, as she failed yet again to sit easily on the stool.

The roly-poly blonde was getting awfully round these days—she could see it just as plain as everyone else. With so little time spent on her feet, what had started as a milfy muffin top had exploded in what felt like overnight! Her belly hung over her nethers, wobbling all the while as her walk slowly became a waddle. With her thick, short legs and her wide hips, Flo toddled more than anything these days!

She knew that she should be cutting back, but she felt like she almost never got to see her friends anymore unless it was Shelby playing hostess—and everything just tasted sooo good…

“Just a few more hours, and then I’m done.” Flo told herself aloud, ripping into the first of two Hardee’s biscuits she’d snagged for herself before work, “At least Shelby’s will be fun tonight…”

***


And then somewhere in between was Dillon Duncan, mulling over about how the best part about being the boss—and running what was essentially an under-the-table business—was that she could pretty much do what she wanted.

And that included wearing sweatpants to work.

She had popped through her last pair of leggings last week—big creamy butt cheeks for all of Shelby’s neighbors to see when she made the fatal mistake of bending over. She’d been getting so big lately that she barely had anything to wear—jeans, slacks, leggings… she’d have even taken jeggings. But instead, all she was left with were the biggest pair of sweatpants that Target had to offer. And in an unflattering insulated gray that only served to make her hips look that much wider.

“Well, I think it’s safe to say I won’t be leaving my office today.” She remarked to herself in a resigned sort of way, grunting as she hefted her stomach out from underneath the waistband of her pants, “Perks of being the boss.”

Dillon pat her pliable flesh derisively—her bombshell figure was long gone, and now so was the fashion. The irony of working in a screen-printing shop and not being able to find clothes that fit her (and fit her well) was not lost on her.

“At least I won’t have to hold back at Shelby’s tonight.” Dillon grunted to herself, shifting on a creaking chair as she turned to her computer, “Since I’m already wearing my Fat Pants.”
111111211.title: Escalation to Enormity

choices: Shelby's not about to quit while she's ahead...
There's still WAY more to do, here...
1111112111.content:
Shelby almost missed the days when she and her friends would galavant around town. The old sitting room had been empty for so long it’d been dusty—and that was just the last time Shelby had set foot through the door. If you would have told her she’d be playing Southern Comfort around the time this whole thing started, she never would have believed it. But there was plenty of joy to be found in playing homemaker.

Especially when you had friends to make you look skinny by comparison. What was a little comfort weight when you had friends like hers rolling up to the dinner table almost every night?

“Hey honey—come on inside, I’ve got everything ready!”

Shelby wrapped her arms around Carrie Cooleyfinger as best she could manage—the woman was getting so big around the belly that she just sort of bumped into her. She couldn’t make her arms meet around unless she hugged that cow around the shoulder anymore, and even that was getting hard with her fat arms and all.

She’d come up to Shelby red-faced, winded from the slight incline in the Sullivan’s yard that ran up the driveway and to the porch. Her puddle cheeks red and blotchy, Carrie sputtered out a response before heading inside. For such a hard-working woman, Carrie was so eager to kick her feet up and relax in the lap of luxury that only her rich friends could provide. The poor thing never saw it coming—Shelby might have felt a little bad for her being blind sighted like this, if the circumstances had been different.

But with things as they were, Carrie was plenty happy to keep coming back—she’d never do anything to upset her friends. Least not the hand that fed her. And that meant Shelby was more than happy to keep on feeding…

Not long after Carrie had come, Flo’s vintage Volkswagen puttered up the side of the road—a four-seater that couldn’t hold anything more than Flo and her daughter as she continued to puff up and up and up…

“Hey sugar…” she gasped, wiping the sweat from underneath her bright blonde bangs, “Good to see you…”

“You too, honey.” Shelby cooed, watching the little ball of blubber waddle up the sidewalk. “Oh, you look so cute.”

Flabby Flo gasped a half-hearted thank you, loathe to admit that she’d had to special order the tent-sized dress that she’d squeezed herself into. The flower pattern distorted along the severe curvature of her gut as it swung between her meaty legs, the only piece of pattern spared was that spread across her bust. Along her mile-wide hips, Flo’s flowers were unrecognizable blobs of lycra spread thin along pasty white flesh.

She was so bottom heavy, Flo barely even waddled. Shelby could hear her thighs chafing from the front porch—how much longer until she was bigger around than she was tall? Watching her kick her legs out at an angle just to get a momentum going, her fat arms swinging stupidly at her side as her belly bobbed and weaved beneath that too-tight lycra dress, Shelby couldn’t help but think that the day was coming sooner rather than later…

“Hff… Hey… pff… Darlin’…”

Dillon was the last to arrive—her husband’s truck failing to dip as low as her shiny BMW did. Such a tall girl would have had hardly any trouble getting in and out of that monster, if she didn’t have the belly weighing her down. Dillon could hardly pick her knees up high enough to see what socks she had on, let alone heft her heifer self in and out of a Dodge without losing balance.

No doubt if she was a shorter woman, Dillon would be having a much harder time getting around than she already was. As big as could be in every sense of the word, Dillon was hit the hardest by Shelby’s scheming. Her plan to turn her friends into fatties so her husband wouldn’t look their way meant that Dillon wasn’t just Tall, she was also getting Big. Well, Bigger. And bigger and bigger and bigger…

Shelby hugged her friend tight, pressing herself into the hanging apron of Dillon’s double-tiered gut. She came up to her friend’s chest, but there was no way she could have gotten that close to the gal anymore. Not without a few lifestyle changes and a very expensive surgery.

Shelby couldn’t help but watch as her friend—a woman “twice as hot as she and Carrie put together” (as her husband had put it once) now more than enough for three women all by her lonesome. Maybe even four!

“Alrighty girls, let’s dig in—” Shelby said once all of her pawns were at the table, “—I made plenty!”

***


“More pie, y’all?”

Troy simply grunted in response, entirely too focused on his macaroni pie to have paid clear attention to what his wife had been rambling on about. Not long ago the mention of Dillon, Flo, and Carrie would have gotten his member throbbing. But now, what with how big they’d all gotten, there wasn’t any hope of that. Not like he could do much good by himself anyway.

“I don’t know how you stay so skinny, Mom.” Summer frowned, her round cheeks creasing as her double chin sank to touch her chest, “I wish I got your metabolism instead of Dad’s.”

“Oh, hush up, it’s just puppy fat.” Shelby crooned as she smacked her daughter’s rippling freckled face playfully, “Now eat up—I made plenty.”

The Sullivans were a big family. With the most important woman in their life so devoted to cooking seemingly twenty-four seven, it was no surprise! With nothing more than a desk job to keep him thin, Troy Sullivan’s former football star figure began to bloat underneath the sheer onslaught of calories his wife kept at an arm’s reach. Even their daughter—former soccer co-captain—was no match! Sluggish on the field as she was in the metabolism department, both Sullivans found themselves just as much at home at the dinner table as their frequent dinner guests…

“So.” Troy grunted, his voice thick and heavy—he was far too fat to get drunk from the two or three Bud’s he’d chugged—“After dinner, what’s say we cuddle up on the couch?”

“Maybe.” Shelby winked, buttering her husband a biscuit, “If you’re good.”

As her husband’s dick throbbed somewhere underneath that belly of his, Shelby’s heart soared in victory. Her husband was too fat to cheat. Her friends were so fat that they’d make anyone look skinny next to them. Everyone was eating out of the palm of her hand—literally at least part of the time!

Life was good. Life was great.

But it could be better.
1111112111.title: Keep Your Friends Close...

choices: Caleb picks up a new stop in his routine—stopping by Cutie Pie's every Wednesday
Caleb wants a little more info on Cat—would it be weird to ask around a bit?
Alternative take where Cat is Fat
Free Choice
12.content:
For most of his life, Caleb Miller had always been far more confident than a man of his stature would have suggested.
As a (mostly) grown man who liked comics, video games, and regularly hosted DnD campaigns online with his similarly nerdy friends, he was surprisingly popular with the “normies”. He was likable, good at his job, and was generally just considered a really fun dude—if a little shy, sort of like the pet introvert. After high school and throughout college, he’d slowly started to come out of his shell more.

Overall, good dude!

If he’d have had one complaint, it’d have been that he’d always been a little on the soft side. That was what held him back. Well, that and a few bad breakups with some high school girlfriends. But who didn’t have baggage from when they were younger? He only ever really had a problem with his weight when it came to comparisons to other dudes. Otherwise he was largely content in his rather sedentary life.

Perhaps a bit too content.

Ever since he’d moved out of his parents’ house and into the dorms, he’d let himself go a little bit. He’d started to wear his hair a little long and he’d managed to grow a respectably bushy beard, but most importantly, there was no one there to reign him in on his worst habits.

Game, snack, sleep. Lunch, comics, sleep. Campaign, snack, game.

He’d come from a comfortably middle class family with an understanding mother and a geeky dad, so they’d never really worked hard on instilling a “stop” switch on their son’s lazy lifestyle. He’d been chubby his whole life anyway, so he was more or less accustomed to the outcome of laying around and snacking all day, but once he hit college and there was no one around to tell him to go to bed or chide him about his eating habits, Caleb started to put on even more weight.

Two hundred and forty pounds of softboi chub, Caleb was like a fluffy marshmallow—and again, he was more or less okay with that. Not great, not horrible, but he ultimately wouldn’t have minded shedding a few extra pounds if he could.

Which he totally could have.

If he was “one of those gym guys.”

It was going through the motions of this sedentary routine, though, that would ultimately change his life for the better. If he hadn’t been there at the comic shop that Wednesday, and if he hadn’t found himself a little hungrier than normal, he might have carried on life as he had been.

But a whiff of peanut butter had caught his attention.

His favorite flavor—how could he not finally check out that bakery? He’d been saying that he’d do it for months—every Wednesday, when he picked up his comics, as a matter of fact. They’d opened up two spaces down from his home away from home, and given his penchant for sweets it was almost a miracle that he hadn’t stopped by before then.

“Cutie Pie’s, huh?”

Putting his comics in the car (until he could bag them and board them later) Caleb walked the parking lot until he was face to face with the glass double doors. Inside was your standard bakery fare—a display counter with all of what they offered, bright LED lights to make everything look pretty and tasty, and a little table with two chairs to sit at. No one was behind the counter, but even from outside he could hear rustling in the back room.

Taking a step inside, the smell of peanut butter became even stronger. A low “mmm” escaped him as he came fully inside the shop, the bell chime sounding above him.

“Hey, welcome to Cutie Pie’s!” a voice sounded from the back, “Be out in just a minute!”

Nodding approvingly, he took a few steps around the counter to examine what had already been prepared. Cupcakes, hand-made donuts, profiteroles and rice krispy bars. There was a fridge with cakes that had been made—presumably by order, judging by the names on them—and a Coca-Cola cooler with Pepsi products inside.

Finally, he came across what had to have been the source of the smell—an empty space for Peanut Butter cookies.

“It’s, uh… gonna be about twenty minutes.” She said in a low voice, “You, um… you maybe wanna hang out here for a while?”

From that day on, Caleb found another addition to his routine—running by Cutie Pie’s after every trip to the comic shop.

Now, he did his best to play it cool. Just because the cute thick bakeshop girl may or may not have been flirting with him, that didn’t mean that he could just spaz out. History had told him that it would have been the exact opposite of how to handle this situation. In fact, why even deal with his feelings at all? She obviously was pretty enough to get anyone she wanted – ten to one odds, he’d find a way to mess this up eventually. She had to have a boyfriend. Maybe she was gay—not that there’s anything wrong with that.

It’s just… you know, girls like that don’t often go for guys like him.

That didn’t stop him from at least trying to make a new friend.

Every Wednesday, after he picked up his comics, he’d run by Cutie Pie’s. Well, run being the operative word. Spending time hanging out in a bakery once, maybe twice a week for a few minutes wasn’t doing him any favors in the weight loss department.

What—he couldn’t just walk in and not buy anything!

That’d let her know that he was here for something other than sweets!

That would have been way too candid!

Most of the time Cat would be working. It was a family bakery, apparently, and Cat was only one of the bakeshop girls. Sometimes it’d be her and her mom, sometimes it’d be her and her aunt, but Caleb always made sure to step in on the off chance that she was there.
He’d still buy something even if she wasn’t though—those ladies knew how to bake!

All of this wouldn’t have meant anything except Caleb needing stretchier pants. As time went on and he continued to wuss out from shooting his shot, he just sort of settled into the life of a frequent bakery customer. He and Cat would talk about school, family drama, and sometimes she’d ask why Caleb kept coming by—always seeming just a little disappointed when he’d say “Well, you know, you’re right next to my favorite comic shop…”

Until one day, when Caleb waddled into the Rancor’s Nest a little late. He’d had to take a makeup test, and that had put him an hour behind schedule.

Normally he’d walk up to the counter and ask for them to pull his box. He’d been coming in for long enough that most of the clerks would see him walking in and pull it for him, but he’d always make a special trip to the counter for his favorite clerks. This time, however, he was met with an invitation to exit as fast as he could

“CALEB, GO NEXT DOOR!”

The tiny, blue-haired clerk Keeley shouted at him over the slow Wednesday ambiance, the lingering six or seven customers around the shop turning to face the commotion. Keeley dropped her conversation with another customer to storm over in an excited frenzy.

“Wh—”

“Bakery Girl came in here looking for you!” Keeley shouted, waving her arms, “She wanted to know if I’d seen you!”

“Keel—”

“I fucking told you that she liked you!” Keeley reached up and poked him in the chest, “Get your fucking nerd books after you go ask her out!”

“B-But I wouldn’t know wha—”

“THEY’RE ABOUT TO FUCKING CLOSE!” the small woman grabbed him by the waist and turned him around, “GO NERD BOY, GO!”

Caleb hurried out the door and sprinted towards the glass doors of Cutie Pie’s Confectionaries. Unfortunately for him, he was hardly in a shape to do so—he’d only gotten doughier since he added their sweets to his already lacking dietary plan. By the time he’d run three store lengths down, he was exhausted. But if Keeley was right, and Cat liked him back… the chafing would be worth it!

Maybe after he caught his breath.

Looking at the time, maybe not.

He opened the door—the soft ding-dong of the overhead chime sounding his arrival. Cat’s long blonde hair fluttered on the other side of the kitchen wall. Her blue eyes practically sparkled at the sight of the chubby nerd gasping his way into their store.

“Hey Caleb!” she said with a smile, “I was startin’ to get worried about you—you’re never late!”

“Had… test…” he panted, “Phew… ran here… fuck…”

“Well, you got here just in time—we’re about to close up shop for the day.” She leaned on the counter, “I was startin’ to think that maybe you’d gotten sick an’ tired of peanut butter cookies.”

“Huff…never…” he managed a weak laugh, “But, uh… but that’s not… that’s not why I’m here.”

Cat frowned cutely.

“So you don’t want your usual?”

“No.” Caleb answered quickly, “I mean, yes actually but… phew… okay…”

Caleb took a deep breath, his boy breasts soaring as his broad chest inflated. Clapping his hands together and letting it out, he fought against the rabid butterflies that tickled his stomach lining. He went against his softboi instincts, stood firm on his wobbling knees, and took his shot.

“Doyoumaybewannagooutsometime?”

And there was a pause—another one of those pauses that had come to define their relationship so far. At first he wasn’t sure if maybe she just hadn’t understood him (he’d been breathing pretty hard) But slowly, a small smile spread across her face.

“Like… as a date?” her apple cheeks were rosy and pink in a rare moment of shyness, “You and me?”

“Yes.” Caleb gasped, his own face an ugly blotchy red, “I, um… I think you’re really cute. But, you know…”

“If, you know, that’dbetooweirdImeanwecanjustpretend—”

It didn’t exactly put him at ease about his weight gain, though. Twenty pounds shy of a hundred in just under a year had to be some kind of record. 12111111.content:
By the time they moved in together, Cat and Caleb had been dating for just under two years.

Senior year had passed and Caleb had graduated with his bachelor’s degree in Communications, meaning that the dorms were officially out of the question for him. Which was good, because he’d started to outgrow them around the time his last semester started.

“Easy there, big boy.”

“Hff… s’fuckin’ heavy, okay?”

Caleb hadn’t felt the pinch of his muscles moving in far too long. It wasn’t often he was even up on his own two feet anymore, instead of just plopped onto whatever couch, bed, or pair of chairs just so happened to have the unfortunate luck of keeping him upright. He waddled blindly towards the front door of their new place—which he was almost too big to fit through—like a big, fat, sputtering locomotive. Anything to get off of his feet.

Caleb’s wide behind wobbled wildly in his shorts, stretched tight over and under the lowermost swell of his billowing gut. His side fat sausaged over the stretchy material like it was casing, giving anyone and everyone who dared to look a clear-cut outline of every roll, fold, and bulge that made up his mountainous physique. Caleb was literally wider than the front door at some points, and he was hardly in any shape to do anything as strenuous as moving.

“Why don’t you go sit down?” Cat asked, short of breath in her own right as she bent over an especially heavy box of clothes, “I think they put everything back in the fridge—maybe get a beer?”

“Fuck yes.”

Caleb’s whole body wobbled as he lumbered hungrily into the kitchen, one booming footstep after the other. He stepped into the kitchen belly-first, exposed by a good few inches as it continued to wriggle its way out of the hammock of the biggest shorts that he could find. His throat was parched, and his appetite calling him. He knew better than anyone that it was time for a snack break…

He had to take a step backwards in order to open the fridge door now, at least if he wanted it open all the way. The cool air felt good against his fat, sweaty body. He’d gotten pretty damp during the ride over, and trying to move those boxes had left him more winded than he would have liked to admit.

He needed to sit down.

Palming a six-pack—it mattered less what was inside, and more that it was cool—and grumbling to himself over the fact that there wasn’t anything tasty to eat in their new place yet, Caleb toddled towards the as-of-yet broken-in couch and collapsed his weight on it.

The whole floor seemed to shake with the impact—had he been on any floor other than the ground level, he might have been concerned.

“Me too babe.” Cat wiped the sweat from her brow and plopped down next to her boyfriend, “All this moving junk is for chumps.”

Cat leaned over the hillside that was her boyfriend to steal a beer for herself. As she reeled herself back in, her tiny belly skidded against Caleb’s overall girth, causing some friction. The small grunt of exertion she sounded fell on deaf ears to the gasping, overheated fat guy sitting next to her, but moving had been tough on Cat too.

“Finally.” She said, leaning deep into the couch as the CRACK of the can filled the apartment, “A place of our own.”

“That isn’t a dorm.”

“I mean, s’not much better than a dorm.” She grunted, “But at least it’s ours.”

Cat sunk deep into the couch, her soft body bunching and bulging as she surrendered to comfort. She ran a hand along her belly as it mushroomed over her tight denim shorts, muffin top jiggling at the slight sensation of her movement. She unbuckled, allowing her tummy to surge forward. Resting a thankful hand on her gut, she sighed contentedly.

“You have no idea how long I’ve been waiting to do that.” She said with a happy, husky tone, “I say we kick back and let the movers worry about all that. We can unpack later.”

“Amen to that.”

Caleb struggled to kick one foot over the other, wriggling towards his girlfriend’s shoulder. Enveloping her in a fleshy side-hug, Caleb held her as close as his body would allow. The heavy breadth of his chest and the thickness of his upper arms meant that Cat was sandwiched between two fleshy rolls. She purred happily and leaned into his belly, one hand running the familiar trail up and down his sagging stomach.

“You’re kinda cute in this new apartment.”

“You’re kinda cute too.”

“Maybe it’s just because you don’t have to worry about any of my family busting in like we do at the store.” Cat said, planting a wet kiss on Caleb’s bushy face, “Or maybe it’s just because you want me to cook you up something for lunch.”

“It can be both.”

“Predictable.” Cat swatted her boyfriend’s belly, taking a moment to watch it jiggle at her touch before grunting to a standing position, “You just want me in the kitchen.”

“I mean… while you are good in other place (cough cough like the bedroom) I do appreciate what you do for me in there.”

“Yeah?” Cat smirked, sauntering back around Caleb’s shoulder and snaking her hand to rub his sagging gut, “Where else do you appreciate me?”

“Literally everywhere—in fact, forget the food, help me up and we can christen the bedroom.”

Caleb gave half-hearted attempts to rise under his own power as Cat laughed. She shushed him with one long pointer finger.

“There’ll be plenty of time for that.” She tittered, heavy chest shaking, “But now, we’re gonna break this place in right—it’s time for lunch.”
12111111.title: Two Lovebirds in a New Nest

choices: With Cat around, Caleb certainly won't be losing any weight any time soon...
Keeley is... unmistakably turned on by all of this...
Free Choice!
121111111.content:
Predictably, Caleb only continued to grow.

He had already been spending most of his time with Cat, who seemed put on to make sure that about eighty percent of that time was spent eating. But ever since they had moved in together, anything further than the impotent thought of moderation had flown entirely out the window as Caleb descended further and further into a pampered life of gluttony and sloth.

He would come home from work (he still worked at the same job, despite his enormous weight) and collapse onto the sofa in his living room. It had become more common than not for him to spend entire days there, barnacling himself to the couch like some kind of overgrown sea urchin while Cat waited on him hand and foot. Everything that his heart desired was his to be had, and he would have been lying if he said that he hadn’t been enjoying it. Food out the wazoo was his favorite way to live, and after two years of dating his doting girlfriend, there was hardly another way for him to live!

Caleb was stretching the limits of what had previously believed to be possible as he toppled five hundred pounds with ease. The longer he and Cat lived together, the bigger that he seemed to get. Six hundred. Six hundred and fifty. Caleb was an eating machine, steadily growing to the size of his new tank like a goldfish. Cat had gotten his hooks into him, and he wasn’t about to let go. Not when everything was so tasty, and the benefits were so juicy…

That being said, he was going to have to start rethinking his shopping strategy.

Just fitting through the door at the Rancor’s Nest was becoming an issue—and they had two.

With Cat on his arm to steady him, Caleb’s entire body trembled with exertion as he waddled inside the comic book store. Panting from the short walk that he’d undergone from here to the car, he pretty much required Cat’s assistance to get around these days. Even a few steps could leave him exhausted, even when there were comics involved.

“Hey Caleb!” Keeley chirped from behind the counter, “Hey Cat!”

“Hi Keeley~” Cat wiggled her fingers at the cashier, “I’m gonna go say hi, okay?”

“Kay…” Caleb puffed weakly as his girlfriend parted from his grasp.

Sauntering forward, it became clear that Caleb wasn’t the only one putting on weight. Surrounding herself with enough food to keep her boyfriend fat and happy hadn’t done the southern sweetheart any favors. She had been noticeably thicker than the other girls in town since they’d met, but in living with Caleb, Cat had opened herself up to a whirlwind of calories that nobody in their life could have prepared for—not even her, considering that she made half of the stuff that he ate.

What resulted was a wide southern belle, with a large set of breasts and flaring birthing hips that sashayed from side to side with every sauntering step. Her thighs fought for dominance in every pair of jeans, shorts, or sweats as she wiggle-waddled her way about town with Caleb up on her arm. Her soft belly bulged out like a muffin in a pan, consistently full and round with whatever snack that she’d often snuck for herself throughout the day she’d spent keeping her boyfriend fed. It had started to hang low into a flabby fupa as she’d gotten chubbier, her close proximity to the fattest guy in town clearly having taking its toll. All that food coming into that apartment… it was only natural that she’d start to snack a little too, right?

She was still beautiful—blue eyed, blonde-haired bombshell that she’d always been. Just a little bigger is all.

Not that she was the only one. Asking for more hours in a shop located next to a good friend’s girlfriend’s bakery shop hadn’t done wonders for Keeley either. The spritely little comic snob was certainly a fair bit thicker than she had been when all of this had started up in the first place. Her belly poured over her leggings and pressed tight against her Redbubble tees—her chubby cheeks dimpling in delight just a little wider for every time Cat gave her a sweet on the house.

They’d gotten to be pretty good friends over the course of the Cat/Caleb relationship—much to one another’s benefit.

“Is he gonna be okay…?” Keeley asked, watching Caleb plod heavily throughout the store, “I don’t mean to pry but… Caleb’s putting on a lot of weight.”

“Yeah, we both are.” Cat said with a sly pat of her belly, giving way to her touch as a slow sloshing motion stemmed from a hearty pat, “I guess that’s just sorta what happens when you get in a relationship with someone who owns a bakery.”

“No shit.” Keeley snorted, “If I wound up with a guy who cooked like you do, I think they’d have to roll me in and out of every shift!”

“What can I say?” Cat winked as she glanced over to her boyfriend, so big that he could barely get around on his own anymore, “The best way to a fella’s heart is through his stomach—and lucky me, Caleb’s heart is just as big as his belly.”

Keeley did the obligatory ‘aww’ that one does when they’re deeply entangled in one friend’s relationship.

“Yeah, we’re cute.” Cat snickered, “Lemme pick up his books and then we’re gonna go out for dinner—it’s almost three years now, can you believe it?”

Keeley took a short, but poigniant look at the man struggling to make his way towards the New Comics wall. She very vaguely remembered a time when he’d only been chubby, but the longer his relationship with Cat went on and as time flew by, it became harder and harder to place just how big he’d always been.

He’d always been kind of big, right?

“I sure as shit can’t.” Keeley said, handing Cat her boyfriend’s bag of comics for the week, “But as long as you two are happy, I guess.”

“Oh, we’re happy alright.” Cat turned and bit her bottom lip at the sight of Caleb leaning against a wall, “Like two pigs in slop.”
121111111.title: Crashing Down into the Comic Shop

choices: Immobility Awaits! (1)
Immobility Awaits! (2)
Free Choice!
1211111111.content:
If Caleb had ever had any reservations about his weight, you never would have known it.

With a loving girlfriend to support and spoil him, one with a passion for cooking that nearly rivaled his affinity and affection for idly stuffing his face, Caleb simply couldn’t stop gaining. His appetite was one that came with weighing hundred and hundreds of pounds, said pounds making it all but impossible for him to exercise. The bigger he got, the hungrier and lazier he got—a vicious cycle of food and immediate gratification that had been ramped up by Cat’s overeager need to please.

Two years out of college, and Caleb had eaten himself so big that he could barely leave the house. Once he took a work-at-home job, he didn’t have to! The hours were easy and he could spend all day on the couch, or at his double-wide reinforced office chair. When he wasn’t on call, he could play games.

It only made sense, as Caleb continued to get even larger, to just move everything to the bedroom. Everything could be brought to him—food, comics, snacks, his laptop, gamer fuel, his switch, all of his meals—Caleb was literally living out his baser instincts come to life.
Cat still worked at the bakery, she’d pick up his comics every Wednesday, and Caleb mostly had the days to himself. He was living the introverted nerd’s dream!

Or at least, some version of it.

As time went on, the memory of how he’d gotten along before he met Cat had faded. They’d been dating for so long now, and so much had changed since they’d gotten together, it was hard for Caleb to remember a time when he wasn’t waited on hand and foot. When he had to wonder what he’d be eating. Whether or not he was attractive to his girlfriend.

Sure, sometimes he’d wonder still—everyone does, now and again.

But Cat had her own unique ways of showing that she was still attracted to what he’d become.

“Sorry again I can’t come to the county fair with you this year.” Caleb said in his deep, husky voice as he panted in bed, “I know it means a lot to you.”

“Don’t you worry about a thing, Caleb—” Cat’s belly sloshed as she waddled heavily over to the side of her boyfriend’s bed, “—you’re doin’ plenty good helpin’ me out just like you are.”

She struggled to plant one of her meaty cheeks on the space next to Caleb’s gut. Even laying in a reclined position, his stomach hung and sagged to compete with the rest of his massive size, and took up plenty of real estate, leaving Cat’s steadily growing behind without much room to call her own.

She planted one boulder on the side of the bed and leaned in to spoon-feed her behemoth boyfriend a bite of pumpkin pie.

“Mmm…”

“Yeah, it’s good?” Cat used her now free hand to make little ripples in Caleb’s gelatinous girth, “You like it better than the last one?”

“I don’t know, last one was pretty good…” Caleb burped, “We got any of that pie left?”

“Not after you got done with it.” Cat tittered, feeding him another bite, “You made short work of that one.”

“I seem to recall you having a slice or two for yourself.” Caleb lifted one waist-thick arm to poke his girlfriend in the belly, “I’m not the only one eating up all the pies around here.”

“Are you implying that your loving, caring, wonderful girlfriend might be gettin’ a teensy bit fat?” Cat cocked an inquisitive blonde eyebrow as she reeled her next readied bite back in

“Oh come on, Kitty Cat—” Caleb’s food-addled brain panicked at the sight of his next meal being whisked away, “—I didn’t mean it like that.”

“Of course you didn’t.” Cat said in a put-on sort of voice as she claimed the bite for herself. And then another. “You’re waaaay too smart to say somethin’ like that in the position that you’re in.”

Another bite, this time slow and sensual. The husky country girl cuddled up close to her beanbag of a boyfriend, their exposed flesh rubbing close together. Caleb’s smothered member twitched somewhere beneath his belly as he began to rub whatever he could reach of his exploded waistline. His whole body had become an erogenous zone after years of dating Cat, who had done her best to love him as hard as every ounce he’d gained over the years. The feeling of her flesh on his was enough to get him riled up and randy—that and maybe the pumpkin pie. The feeding for sure. In fact, Caleb wasn’t sure what about his situation with Cat hadn’t turned him on.

Even the extra weight looked good on her—not that he was one to judge someone for putting on a little comfort weight in a relationship.
Cat leaned forward and spooked him another bite, tracing the wet pink lips beneath his beard. The bed squeaked as she wriggled her way up on her knees—tit flesh pouring onto Caleb’s mattress of a chest as she struggled to maintain a balance.

“Open wide, cutie pie~” Cat purred, leaning close as Caleb stared longingly at his girlfriend and his next bite…

It would have been something right out of his fantasies—food and Cat—if it hadn’t been for the boxspring cracking beneath more than one thousand pounds of pressure. The two of them suddenly lurched towards the ground, and Cat came tumbling down onto her back.

Luckily, they were both well-insulated for impact.

After the commotion died down and everyone made sure that everyone else was alright, Caleb was the one who had deduced what had happened after noticing that the TV sat a full foot higher than he was used to—he had to crane his neck a little bit, and even the slightest discomfort had become utterly foreign enough for him to notice.

“Looks like we broke the boxspring.” He said dourly, “Damn, Cat… maybe we ought to think about losing some weight.”

“I… think I have a better idea…” Cat said, far more aroused at the idea of a broken box spring than anyone had any right to be, “One that involves a lot more pie.”
1211111111.title: King-Sized Caleb

choices: An option to continue past the main story... (1)
