Despite the dismissing notion that she would start to curb her appetite after she got out of college, Haley’s lacking enthusiasm for exercise and moderation made for an empty promise. Her mother had been as proud as she could be seeing her daughter waddle across the stage in her graduation robes, and that pride had been so overwhelming that she was happy to give Haley a little more leeway as far as finding a job went. In this economy, and as an English major, it was going to be tough for her to find a job that she was going to like—why kick her out the door when she wasn’t ready?

But as May ran into June and then July, now they were into August, and Haley had still shown no sign of finding employment. After four years of laying around on her mother’s dime, eating all she wanted and getting what she needed, Haley was more than happy to extend her time with her Mom for as long as possible!

“Mornin’…”

Haley’s thunderous footsteps seemed to shake the whole house, the décor trembling as Haley waddled gut-first into the living room and towards the kitchen. A smorgasbord of food awaiting her like always—nothing she didn’t deserve, naturally! She was a college graduate now!

For the past few weeks.

Haley had spent her teenaged years prowling around Westgate Mall thinking that it was the coolest shit. But now that she was in her mid-twenties and almost nothing had changed (besides the fact that they got rid of that bomb ass arcade for another stupid clothing store that didn’t have anything that fit her!) “…so the chair turned into a whole big thing about and, long story short—” Haley slurped on her straw before tossing the emptied sweet tea into the trash, “—now my mom’s bitching at me about getting a job.”

What was there to even do as an English major in South Carolina? Haley sure as hell hadn’t found out in her three months of barely looking, and hadn’t turned up anything new in the week or so that she’d spent searching ever since she got in a fight with her mom.

And while it wasn’t exactly likely that they’d have any cool band t-shirts that Haley could squeeze into (at least on the rack) it was sort of a tradition that every time she came to Westgate Mall she’d stop by Hot Topic. “I’m here because… whatever, okay?” Dani sniffed as she cut into her first of three Greek-style chicken breasts, “Maybe I… maybe I fucking missed you guys.”

“Awwwww~”

“Shut up—do you want your fucking cheeseburger or not?”

Haley had never really thought much about why she and Dani had been friends in the first place. Truth be told, she wasn’t entirely sure that the pudgy goth could stand her. Seeing her as big as she as, it almost filled her with a sense of… it was hard to say. Pride? Maybe that she hadn’t managed to be the only person to blow up so big? Or maybe in the sense that she was still smaller than someone else? Maybe it was happiness—she and Dani had been friends for a while (apparently) and having Courtney constantly dip in and out of her life while her mom was starting to harp on her for getting a job… it was nice.

Big Daddy’s had a hard closing time of ten o clock in the evening, which meant that the two of them had a lot of eating to do if they wanted to get out of there in time for Petro and the girls to close up shop. Fortunately, neither of them were exactly familiar with the word restraint. Eating was second nature to the two southern sweethearts as they swelled over the table, happy to talk about old times. Haley caught Dani up on Courtney (and her stupid diet!) and did her best to avoid asking just what the fuck had happened to make Dani so goddamn fat.

They had been having a great time, truthfully—a rarity for the two of them, together. Given Dani’s general animosity towards Haley, it was almost surprising that the two of them could sit down together and have a cordial (if large) meal. Haley had been eased into a sense of security (though that may have been the food) and wasn’t at all suspecting just what was going to come out of Dani’s mouth next:

“So… are you down to smoke or something?”

Haley nodded her chubby little head yes. It had been a while since she’d partaken in the Devil’s Lettuce—it had never been her favorite party favor, always opting for the alcohol. But she’d never turned it down before, even if it did mean some more awkward face-timing with Dani. After all, it wasn’t like she had to wake up early in the morning for a job…

“Cool, cool… are you wearing that, or do you want to change?”

“…I’m sorry, what?”

“Nevermind, just…” Dani scrunched her face a little, “Whatever, help me out of this fucking booth.”
1411211111111114111111111.title: Old Stuffing Grounds, Again

choices: "Hey Haley, what do you think of Dani?"
"Hey Haley... you're single, right?"
"Hey Haley, still got some room left?"
14112111111111141111111111.content:
At the time, it had seemed like an odd question for Dani to ask.

“Are you wearing that, or do you want to change?”

Who asks that before you go smoke with someone? It’s just not a thing that you think to ask when you’re about to spark up a jay. Haley had just written it off as Dani being Dani. Once a gloomy goth girl, always a gloomy goth girl, despite tripling in size since she’d seen her last. Extra weight wasn’t the only thing that Dani had picked up though—she now sported two roommates that had been sitting in the living room when she and Dani walked into the house just off of Heron Circle.

The whole house was pretty dank. It was obvious how they’d intended to spend their night. Like Dani, Joni and Michelle had something of an alternative look to them. Where Dani was more dedicated to the Goth Girl Aesthetic ™, her roommates were more varied in style and in size. Joni had a more ambitiously neon coloration to her hair and clothing, was short and kind of big, where Michelle was just sort of… ordinary looking? (Was that the right way to say it?) Neither fat nor especially noteworthy, she was in an old holey band t-shirt and had been mid-rip of the bong when Dani managed to squeeze through the doorway.

Dani had led her to the couch, made a half-assed introduction (“this is Haley, we went to school together, she’s gonna smoke with us.”) and plopped down with a foundation-shaking kaboom next to her. The furniture had to be reinforced. Haley hadn’t put much thought into just how much she weighed until she’d started snapping furniture, but Dani was way bigger than her, and that couch was holding the two of them up…

Luckily for everyone involved, Haley wasn’t going to be doing much thinking tonight.

The little circle that had gathered wasn’t much more sophisticated than anything that Haley had experienced in high school—four girls passing around a bong, plenty of snacks, TV blaring. The only thing that was different was that no one’s parents were going to be home, and the alcohol was totally legal. And, just like back when she was in high school, Haley was a total lightweight—she hadn’t danced with the Devil’s Lettuce in years, and it showed in her stupid red-faced giggling. With the added appeal of alcohol, the bottom on Haley quickly fell out as she lowered herself into the couch for the night.

Unsurprisingly, Haley ate a lot.

She was already a self-admitted snack hog. Dani commanded the central flow of any and all things edible that were to be passed along the circle’s track, which put Haley in a precariously piggish position of being right next to the greediest girl in the room. Even without being stoned off her big round ass or drunk enough to fail a breathalyzer two yards away, would have definitely pigged out on the primo snack stash that Dani and her roommates brought out for their reunion smoke special.

Dani expertly worked her thigh-thick arms through the rotation of smoke, swig, snack while Haley struggled to keep up. She had the passion, but lacked the stamina. Engrossed in her vices, she barely contributed to the pitiful conversation of faux-deep philosophy that the other three managed. As time slowed down and Haley’s attentions narrowed in on whatever was placed in front of her, Haley could practically feel herself puffing up, and up, and up with every bite…

“You okay Haley?”

“M’feelingreattttt”

Haley pushed a heaping handful of Flaming Hot Cheetos into her mouth as she reclined against the back of the couch, her stomach surging outwards as it eeked out from underneath her shirt. Her fat cheeks full and flush, she turned to the source of the inquisition—Dani, sitting next to her in all of her pale, mountainous glory.

Her head was swimmy and stupid, filled with the nothingness that came with being high off her ass and drunk as a skunk at the same time. She was giggly and dumb, her chubby cheeks bouncing with laughter as she sunk slowly into Dani’s larger form. Almost like a moon succumbing to orbit.

She hadn’t been paying particularly good attention when somebody said it, so she wasn’t quite sure as to whether or not it was Joni or Michelle, but somebody said—
14112111111111141111111111.title: Hotbox Snackbox

choices: Haley wakes up the next morning, and is a little peeved...
Haley wakes up the next morning... in bed with Dani.
Free Choice
141121111111111411111111111.content:
“Hey Haley, what do you think of Dani?”

And it seemed like such an odd question. Haley, even high off her plump rump, wasn’t sure that she’d heard it right. What did she think of Dani—what did anyone think of Dani?

“Hey Haley, wanna be a part of a guest shoot for Dani’s OnlyFans?”

Haley snorted.

“Wut?”

There was a little bit of laughter at Dani’s expense as the mountainous goth blob pat her pale stomach, unsheathed to the world at large. She handled it on both ends with her chubby hands, giving the incredible girth a small jiggling motion with her chubby fingers. The smallest smile, a rarity for Dani even now, spread across her lips as she stared into Jodie’s camera phone.

“Yeah, not like… sex or anything, but maybe just like make out a little.”

Haley looked at Dani, and then at Jodie and Michelle, and then back again at Dani.

“I meannnnnnn…”

Haley’s head was still more than a little swimmy. This was good stuff they had here.

“We’ll give you some more weed?”

“And order a pizza.” Jodie added, almost hungrily, “Come onnnnnn…”

Haley’s gears spun without grinding as she did her best to contemplate the moral dilemma that she found herself in while high off her ass. On the one hand, making out with Dani. On the other, free weed and pizza.

Sometimes, it was better not to think too hard about these kinds of decisions.
141121111111111411111111111.title: "Hey Haley, what do you think of Dani?"

choices: This isn't such a bad idea...
This is a HORRIBLE idea...
This is a Free Choice!
1411211111111114111111111111.content:
“What the fuck, Dani?”

Waking up the next morning hadn’t been fun. On top of the unfamiliar sensation that came with sleeping over at anyone’s house, Haley had woken up to (in order) a pounding head, a bag of complementary breakfast burritos that were starting to get cold, and the distinct impression that she’d done something a little gross the night before.

When Dani had shown her the video, it all came back to Haley in a flash of dank, cookie-filled drama.

“What happened to not everybody wants to jump your bones?”

Watching the video again, this time with sober eyes, and Haley was more than a little miffed at the implications of what she’d done. And that the pizza had been cheap, and didn’t sit right on her stomach. And that she had a headache from coughing too much…

Haley was just having a bad morning. And she was going to do everything within her power to make sure that Dani had a pretty shitty morning too.

“I didn’t jump your bones, Jesus.” Dani’s fat cheeks bounced slightly as she ate her breakfast burrito, “We made out a little bit for the camera—don’t tell me that you’ve never kissed a girl at a party before?”

Haley’s face turned a deeper shade of red as she refused to dignify Dani’s question with a response. Her anger made her sputter stupidly as the words just seemed to fail to come to her in this, the most confusing moment of her life. Waking up in a strange house with two girls who she’d never seen before and a massive pile of goth that Haley could only just barely recognize as her old friend Dani. Add into the fact that she had just… showed her the video as if it were the most ordinary thing in the world…

“Shut up!” Haley pouted, “Is that why you asked me over last night?”

“Noooooo…” Dani clicked her tongue, “I mean… not like, just that…”

“That was your chance to lie about this, you big dumb bitch!”

Dani grumbled and rolled her eyes, set deep in her marshmallow cheeks as she reclined in her seat on the couch. The gothoppotamus seemed even bigger than she did last night, when Haley was all goofed up on the Devil’s Lettuce. She looked like a pile of pillows, crammed into a t-shirt fit for a circus tent. Her fat legs splayed to either side, bean bag stomach settling nicely over the rim of the poor overtaxed sofa’s cushion. Leaning forward to talk with Haley, and making her way through her breakfast, had left her with the slightest pant to her breath.

She reached into the bag that Michelle had brought her (it paired nicely with Haley’s smaller one) and began to unwrap another burrito.

“Look, whatever, if you want to bitch about it okay, but I knew that you’d be a hit.” Dani’s singular flap of neck rippled slightly as she spoke, “So you can thank me for making you Internet Famous or you can just cunt on out of here.”

Haley was about to slap her across the speedbags that she called cheeks, but with some small struggle, Dani’s hand plunged deep into her bra. She handed Haley a crinkled, damp pair of twenties.

“That’s how much money you made me from that video last night.” Dani said in a stern voice, “You calling me fat and making out with me—rubbing my belly and shit while I eat Oreos? My fans eat it up.”

“…what the fuck, Dani?” Haley asked, looking at the money

“You were talking to me last night about how your mom was all pissed off at you and you needed to get a job, right?” Dani’s tone didn’t soften, though she didn’t seem as angry as she had been when Haley was priming herself to slap her, “Well, in for like a two-minute video you probably made enough money to buy another stupid chair.”

There was a small, deescalating pause between the two of them as Haley began to put two and two together. Holding the damp boob money in her hands, Haley held it a little closer between her fingers.

“You… do this?” Haley asked, a hint of judgement in her voice, “And you… get views? You get paid?”

“What? Is it really that hard to believe?”

Haley gave a cursory glance towards the mountain of woman piled onto the couch. Pale white, with her makeup still on from the night before, Dani wasn’t without a certain… charm (was that the right word to describe whatever it was that Dani had going on?) but it was a little hard to believe that someone like her could make money in this line of work.

"This line of work"—it sounded so seedy to think about it that way.

“Lots of people like fat chicks, Haley” Dani said with a little smirk, her hand snaking down to wobble a handful of ivory blubber overhang, “This is what pays for my share of the rent.”

As if to make a point, Dani bit deeply into her breakfast burrito. A soft belch sounded from her lips as she smacked them in satisfaction. Balling up the tinfoil, she reached into the bag and grabbed another. And then another.

“If you’re having money issues, I could help you set up your account.” Dani offered between bites, “You know, get you started. Make a few guest appearances?”

“Th…This is real.” Haley made a face, “This is a conversation that we’re having, right now.”

“Fine, fuck off then.” Dani snorted, “But don’t come crying to me when you hate your lame-ass job… if you ever fucking find one in Spartanburg-fuckin’-County.”

Haley sort of stood there, contemplating just what it was that Dani was saying. Offering. Basically telling her to do. It was true, Haley’s (minimal, basically nonexistant) efforts at finding employment hadn’t yielded anything so far. And her options were a lot more limited than she would have liked to admit. And… you know, as long as it wasn’t anything gross…

What was the harm in a little easy money?
1411211111111114111111111111.title: A New Direction?

choices: Put on the Red Light
Gimme Shelter
A Respectable Size
One Little Bite
15.content:
Put on the Red Light—

Harper Black is having the worst time of her life. Just over forty with three kids and no man in her life, she's all but hit rock bottom emotionally by the time a chance encounter with one of Daven's Port's Finest turns her life around.

Officer Roxanne Reagan is everything that she's ever wanted in a lover! Caring, doting, charming... Harper is finding herself having something of a midlife crisis by the time she realizes that she's a bit more bicurious than she thought!

However, there seems to be more to Roxanne than she's letting on. She's TOO perfect. TOO willing to let Harper indulge herself... what's her game?


Gimme Shelter—

Kim Rhodes won a contest... and on the radio no less.

It gives her an all-expenses-paid trip to the Eloi Resort, literally one of the most luxurious places in the world!

Unfortunately, it doesn't seem like they're all that keen on letting her leave.


A Respectable Size—

Erin has a problem. It's that she isn't taken seriously in the corporate world.

And how fucking stupid is that, right? Like, she's climbed her way up the corporate ladder, done everything in her power to make these people respect her, and now she's getting shafted just because she's a woman!

Well, she'll show them. If there's one thing that the Chads and Chrises in this type of environment understand, it's intimidation. And what's more intimidating than someone who can literally crush them with her ass?



One Little Bite—

For most of her daughter's life, Donna has been just a hair's length controlling.

It's not her fault. You don't know what it's like to have their genes. Everything that she's done to keep junk food and sweets out of the house has been for Delaney's own good. She didn't want her daughter growing up getting the same treatment she had as "Double Wide Donna"

So you can imagine her frustration when Delaney picks up Culinary Arts as her elective.

info.description:
I get a lot of requests and suggestions for the stories that I write.

And who can blame people for asking? There are really popular characters who steal the show, who deserve their own chance to shine. There are plot lines that I would love to explore to their fullest potential, even if it would take the story way off of the rails! Sometimes, I just want a do-over!

This is my answer to fan requests—an interactive hub where things aren't so tightly constrained by the narrative!

Please enjoy these previously non-interactive stories, reformatted for your leisure! Feel free to jump in and take the reins and flesh out as many ideas (and characters) as you want!
